resourceOrchestration.title: Resource-native orchestration
resourceOrchestration.description: Declare Workers, Experts, Workflows, prompts, models, tools, knowledge, and compute as versioned resources. The control plane validates references, shows a semantic plan, and applies an immutable revision or execution snapshot.
resourceOrchestration.lifecycleDescription: The workflow separates authoring from review and execution. A Plan is evidence of the intended change, not proof that the resource already exists.

validate.title: Validate
validate.description: Check the envelope, schema, identifiers, field semantics, permissions, and referenced resources.

plan.title: Plan
plan.description: Produce a canonical manifest, semantic diff, blocking issues, warnings, and pinned reference digests.

apply.title: Apply
apply.description: Reauthorize the actor, reject stale plans, consume the plan once, and create the typed revision or domain object.

bindings.name: Binding resources
bindings.purpose: Version references to models, tools, repositories, skills, knowledge, secrets, compute targets, and resource profiles.

prompt.name: Prompt
prompt.purpose: Store reusable prompt content and declared variables as an immutable revision.

workerTemplate.name: WorkerTemplate
workerTemplate.purpose: Compile reusable runtime, model, workspace, and lifecycle configuration into a WorkerSpec snapshot.

worker.name: Worker
worker.purpose: Launch one Worker from pinned resources. The same Worker resource cannot be updated.

expertWorkflow.name: Expert / Workflow
expertWorkflow.purpose: Create reusable domain definitions with pinned WorkerTemplate, Prompt, and WorkerSpec snapshots.

goalLoop.name: GoalLoop
goalLoop.purpose: Validate and plan a goal-driven loop; typed resource Apply is not exposed yet.

resourceOrchestration.referencesDescription: Resources reference one another by kind, name, namespace, and optional revision. Omitting revision means resolve the active revision while planning, not follow the latest revision forever.
resourceOrchestration.referencesPinned: The Plan pins apiVersion, kind, namespace, name, UID, revision, and digest. Apply and later execution use that fixed result.
resourceOrchestration.editorDescription: The domain form and YAML editor operate on one typed draft. Invalid YAML remains visible and blocks form switching, planning, and applying; the editor never continues with an older valid draft.

steps.form: Use the form for ordinary fields and resource selectors.
steps.yaml: Use YAML for review, version control, or precise advanced editing.
steps.validate: Resolve schema, permission, identifier, and reference errors.
steps.plan: Review CREATE or UPDATE, semantic diff, warnings, revisions, and digests.
steps.apply: Apply only the current plan and verify the returned revision, snapshot, domain object, or Worker.

resourceOrchestration.securityDescription: Resource YAML describes references, not plaintext credentials. Namespace is an identity field and never replaces authorization.

items.credentials: ModelBinding stores a model resource ID; provider credentials remain encrypted in the AI resource connection.
items.authorization: Validate, Plan, and Apply reject cross-organization or unreadable references, including permissions revoked after planning.
items.stale: Draft changes, expired plans, consumed plans, changed heads, and changed runtime catalogs require a new Plan.

resourceOrchestration.goalLoopBoundary: GoalLoop supports Validate and Plan, but no typed Apply endpoint is exposed. A successful Plan does not create a loop.
resourceOrchestration.reconciliationBoundary: This model is declarative but does not run a Kubernetes-style reconciliation loop. Deleting a Worker or Pod does not recreate it automatically.
